description: 'muro' describes persistence in a position or course after reasons to reconsider have appeared. A person may be stubborn about a small preference or a public decision. 'kesu' (determined) keeps effort aimed at a purpose; 'muro' makes resistance to adjustment part of the description.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm', carry the voice through the nose, then raise the back of the tongue and round the lips for stressed high-back 'u'. Make a tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge for 'r' and finish with true-mid rounded back 'o'.
sound_symbolism: The lips close at the start, both vowels remain rounded, and the brief tap changes the articulation without opening the form widely. In Phi, that contained motion can echo a position held past the moment of easy adjustment.
usage_notes: A speaker can apply 'muro' to their own position as readily as another person's. Context supplies whether the resistance seems useful or costly.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: When someone holds a position after the reasons have changed, 'muro' gives Buddhist practice a plain word for attachment to a view.

semantic_domains.activity: persistence in a course despite reasons to adjust it
semantic_domains.cognition: resistance to reconsidering a position or judgement
